generatedSentence: A known international technician, usually nonchalant, found himself working late on a majorly complex lunar lattice update. His cheeky kids had left leftover trashy items, a prank that caused minor entropy. He needed to redo calculations, his muscles aching. Vibrations from a loose gasket hummed, threatening to unjoin the system. He tweeted a verbose researcher for help, noting a faint watermark on old bricks. He felt the tension rising. The researcher's reply was brusque: a cryptic message about a windigo and a malevolent vigilante. Nightmares of a murderous vulture, its tongue out and pucker forming a grotesque image, had haunted the technician. A nearby elevator suddenly unjoined, its fanjets buzzing. He saw a keyhole symbol on an azulejo tile, a clue. Was this unlikely scenario some elaborate hijinks, or the result of a scuzzbag iconoclast? His cortisol spiked; his intellect, usually blunt, now sharpened. The windigo, an adjoint entity, materialized onto the overhang, its silhouette against the sunny sky. It had been kneecapped and injures were visible, yet it moved with surprising charisma. It was knifing open the turret, its knife glinting. The sounds were a ruckus, like deejays gone bonkers. The technician, now a finder, had to rescue his kids. He saw a vague, looping 'U' symbol, like a watermark, and realized this was a major entrapment. He needed a strategy, not more drooling fear. With a sudden, unilateral grapple, the technician made his move, his buzzcut head clearing the bricks. He used a flamethrower from a hidden refuge, the heat causing the windigo to deflate. The resultant gains were immense, a serendipity of action. He huzzahed loudly, a wave of satisfaction replacing nausea. His actions were a metaphor for jailbreaks, freeing his family. He tabulate his findings on a checkbox, determined to texturize the details for the administrator, ensuring no leftover entropy. Dinner would be next, followed by a well-deserved opal-like calm.

The researcher's reply was brusque: a cryptic message about a windigo and a malevolent vigilante. The researcher's reply was brusque: a cryptic message about a windigo and a malevolent vigilante. The windigo, an adjoint entity, materialized onto the overhang, its silhouette against the sunny sky. It had been kneecapped and injures were visible, yet it moved with surprising charisma. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone technician, wearing a simple gray jumpsuit with glowing blue kneepads and a worn buzzcut. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The technician, focused intently on a holographic screen displaying a complex lattice, a small, mischievous child peeking from behind a trashy leftover item, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The technician recoiling in shock, a ghostly, shadowy windigo with glowing red eyes visible through a keyhole in a cracked azulejo tile, medium close-up, Dutch angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The windigo, a gaunt, imposing figure, perched on a futuristic overhang, a large, ornate turret being pried open with a glowing knife, a child's silhouette visible inside, dramatic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The technician, mid-grapple with the windigo, a burst of orange flame from a flamethrower engulfing the creature, children safely running away in the background, dynamic action shot.

definitions.definition: Something left behind; an excess or remainder.
definitions.example: It's a leftover from yesterday, but it's still perfectly good.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural, usually of food) Remaining after a meal is complete or eaten for a later meal or snack.
definitions.example: Not leftovers again!

definitions.definition: Remaining; left behind; extra; in reserve.
definitions.example: Do you want some of the leftover supplies from the event?

definitions.definition: A device that projects a flame for starting fires, and sometimes also additional fuel to help ignition. Used either as a weapon or a tool.
A translucent design impressed on the surface of paper and visible when the paper is held to the light.
(by extension) A logo superimposed on a digital image, a television broadcast, etc.
A value stored in a datafile to ensure its integrity, so that if the file's contents are changed then the watermark will no longer match the contents.
A mark indicating the level to which water has risen.
To mark paper with a watermark.
To mark a datafile with a digital watermark.
A hybrid fish derived from a male brook trout and a female lake trout
A malevolent and violent cannibal spirit found in Anishinaabe, Ojibwe, and Cree mythology, which is said to inhabit the body of a living person and possess him or her to commit murder.
Like trash; containing much trash
definitions.example: a trashy novel

definitions.definition: Having a sound like white noise
definitions.example: trashy cymbals

definitions.definition: A demon or monster, thought to plague people while they slept and cause a feeling of suffocation and terror during sleep.
Sleep paralysis.
A very bad or frightening dream.
definitions.example: I had a nightmare that I tried to run but could neither move nor breathe.

definitions.definition: Any bad, miserable, difficult or terrifying situation or experience that arouses anxiety, terror, agony or great displeasure.
definitions.example: Cleaning up after identity theft can be a nightmare of phone calls and letters.

definitions.definition: A contractile form of tissue which animals use to effect movement.
definitions.example: Muscle consists largely of actin and myosin filaments.

definitions.definition: An organ composed of muscle tissue.
(usually in the plural) A well-developed physique, in which the muscles are enlarged from exercise.
Strength, force.
Hired strongmen or bodyguards.
To use force to make progress, especially physical force.
definitions.example: He muscled his way through the crowd.

definitions.definition: Of, characterized by, or pertaining to murder or murderers.
Of a person: intending or likely to commit murder; bloodthirsty, homicidal.
definitions.example: She gave me a murderous look.

definitions.definition: Of an object: used to commit murder; capable of causing death; deadly, fatal.
(by extension) Very difficult.
definitions.example: a murderous exam

definitions.definition: Anything that raises or uplifts.
A permanent construction with a built-in platform that is lifted vertically, used to transport people and goods.
A silo used for storing wheat, corn or other grain (grain elevator)
A control surface of an aircraft responsible for controling the pitching motion of the machine.
A dental instrument used to pry up ("elevate") teeth in difficult extractions, or depressed portions of bone.
Any muscle that serves to raise a part of the body, such as the leg or the eye.
A type of shoe having an insert lift to make the wearer appear taller.
Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A measure of the disorder present in a system.
definitions.example: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.

definitions.definition: The capacity factor for thermal energy that is hidden with respect to temperature http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055.

The dispersal of energy; how much energy is spread out in a process, or how widely spread out it becomes, at a specific temperature. https//web.archive.org/web/20060702234316/http://www.entropysite.com/students_approach.html
A measure of the amount of information and noise present in a signal.
The tendency of a system that is left to itself to descend into chaos.
(of a function) Assuming each of the values in its codomain; having its range equal to its codomain.
definitions.example: Considered as a function on the real numbers, the exponential function is not onto.

definitions.definition: Upon; on top of.
definitions.example: My cat just jumped onto the keyboard.

definitions.definition: Aware of.
definitions.example: The thought-police were onto my plans of world domination.

definitions.definition: Being an onto function with a codomain of (see below).
definitions.example: The exponential function maps the set of real numbers onto the set of positive real numbers.

definitions.definition: The volume that tips the balance between the demand and the supply toward demand lagging supply.
That portion of the roof structure that extends beyond the exterior walls of a building.
A fatty roll of pubis flab that hangs over one's genitals; a FUPA.
Anything that overhangs or protrudes over its base, such as a wave immediately before breaking, or a protruding cliff or rock wall.
To hang over (something).
To impend.
A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay etc., used for building.
definitions.example: This wall is made of bricks.

definitions.definition: Such hardened mud, clay, etc. considered collectively, as a building material.
definitions.example: This house is made of brick.

definitions.definition: Something shaped like a brick.
definitions.example: a plastic explosive brick

definitions.definition: A helpful and reliable person.
definitions.example: Thanks for helping me wash the car. You're a brick.

definitions.definition: A shot which misses, particularly one which bounces directly out of the basket because of a too-flat trajectory, as if the ball were a heavier object.
definitions.example: We can't win if we keep throwing up bricks from three-point land.

definitions.definition: A power brick; an external power supply consisting of a small box with an integral male power plug and an attached electric cord terminating in another power plug.
An electronic device, especially a heavy box-shaped one, that has become non-functional or obsolete.
A carton of 500 rimfire cartridges, which forms the approximate size and shape of a brick.
A community card (usually the turn or the river) which does not improve a player's hand.
definitions.example: The two of clubs was a complete brick on the river.

definitions.definition: The colour brick red.
One kilo of cocaine.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: The act of vibrating or the condition of being vibrated.
Any periodic process, especially a rapid linear motion of a body about an equilibrium position.
A single complete vibrating motion.
An instinctively sensed emotional aura or atmosphere; vibes.
One who administers affairs; one who directs, manages, executes, or dispenses, whether in civil, judicial, political, or ecclesiastical affairs; a manager
A person who manages or settles the estate of an intestate, or of a testator when there is no competent executor; one to whom the right of administration has been committed by competent authority
One who is responsible for software installation, management, information and maintenance of a computer or network
An advisement providing more up-to-date information than currently known.
definitions.example: He gave me an update on the situation in New York.

definitions.definition: A change in information, a modification of existing or known data.
definitions.example: I just made an update to the Wikipedia article on guerillas.

definitions.definition: An additional piece of information. An addition to existing information.
definitions.example: I just made an update to my blog about my trip to Rome.

definitions.definition: A modification of something to a more recent, up-to-date version; (in software) a minor upgrade.
definitions.example: I have a couple of updates to install on your laptop.

definitions.definition: A version of something which is newer than other versions.
definitions.example: You should try the update: it rocks.

definitions.definition: To bring (a thing) up to date.
definitions.example: I need to update my records to take account of the most recent transaction.

definitions.definition: To bring (a person) up to date: to inform (a person) about recent developments.
definitions.example: Update me on what happened while I was away.

definitions.definition: To separate or detach (things that were joined).
To cease to be a member of; to leave.
To fasten using a peg.
definitions.example: Let's peg the rug to the floor.

definitions.definition: To affix or pin.
definitions.example: I found a tack and pegged your picture to the bulletin board.

definitions.definition: To fix a value or price.
definitions.example: China's currency is no longer pegged to the American dollar.

definitions.definition: To narrow the cuff openings of a pair of pants so that the legs take on a peg shape.
To throw.
To indicate or ascribe an attribute to. (Assumed to originate from the use of pegs or pins as markers on a bulletin board or a list.)
definitions.example: He's been pegged as a suspect.

definitions.definition: To move one's pegs to indicate points scored; to score with a peg.
definitions.example: She pegged twelve points.

definitions.definition: To reach or exceed the maximum value on (a scale or gauge).
definitions.example: We pegged the speedometer across the flats.

definitions.definition: (typically in heterosexual contexts) To engage in anal sex by penetrating (one's male partner) with a dildo.
To keep working hard at something; to peg away.
A practical joke or mischievous trick.
definitions.example: He pulled a gruesome prank on his sister.

definitions.definition: An evil deed; a malicious trick, an act of cruel deception.
To perform a practical joke on; to trick.
To call someone's phone and promptly hang up
definitions.example: Hey man, prank me when you wanna get picked up.

definitions.definition: To adorn in a showy manner; to dress or equip ostentatiously.
To make ostentatious show.
Full of gambols or tricks.
The middle bone of the proximal series of the carpus in the wrist, which is shaped like a half-moon.
An observation of a lunar distance, especially for establishing the longitude of a ship at sea.
Of, pertaining to, or resembling the Moon (that is, Luna, the Earth's moon); Lunar.
definitions.example: lunar observations  a lunar eclipse

definitions.definition: Shaped like a crescent moon; lunate.
(Believed to be) influenced by the Moon, as in character, growth, or properties.
Of or pertaining to silver (which was symbolically associated with the Moon by alchemists).
Of or pertaining to travel through space between the Earth and the Moon, or exploration and scientific investigation of the Moon.
A flat panel constructed with widely-spaced crossed thin strips of wood or other material, commonly used as a garden trellis.
A bearing with vertical and horizontal bands that cross each other.
A regular spacing or arrangement of geometric points, often decorated with a motif.
A discrete subgroup of Rn which is isomorphic to Zn (considered as an additive group) and spans the real vector space Rn.
A model of the tuning relationships of a just intonation system, comprising an array of points in a periodic multidimensional pattern.
(Lie theory) A discrete subgroup L of a given locally compact group G whose quotient space G/L has finite invariant measure.
A partially ordered set in which every pair of elements has a unique supremum and a unique infimum.
To make a lattice of.

definitions.definition: To close, as an opening, with latticework; to furnish with a lattice.

definitions.definition: A length of rope used for reefing a sail, or holding a stowed sail in place.
Any mechanical seal that serves to fill the space between two objects, generally to prevent leakage between the two objects while under compression.
A material which may be clamped between faces and acts as a static seal. Gaskets may be cut, formed, or molded to the desired configuration.
Any of a wide variety of seals or packings used between matched machine parts or around pipe joints to prevent the escape of a gas or fluid.
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: Any fact or situation which is known or familiar.
A constant or variable the value of which is already determined.
Identified as a specific type; famous, renowned.

definitions.definition: Accepted, familiar, researched.
A person who studies or practises technology.
The sound of a bird; any short high-pitched sound or whistle.
An entry posted on the microblogging service Twitter.
To make a short high-pitched sound, like that of certain birds.
To post an update to Twitter.
An onomatopoeic of bird singing.
A vertical tube or hollow column used to emit environmentally polluting gaseous and solid matter (including but not limited to by-products of burning carbon or hydrocarbon based fuels); a flue.
The glass flue surrounding the flame of an oil lamp.
The smokestack of a steam locomotive.
A narrow cleft in a rock face; a narrow vertical cave passage.
To negotiate a chimney (narrow vertical cave passage) by pushing against the sides with back, feet, hands, etc.
The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans and some other animals.
definitions.example: When I'm nervous I bite my nails.

definitions.definition: The basal thickened portion of the anterior wings of certain hemiptera.
The terminal horny plate on the beak of ducks, and other allied birds.
The claw of a bird or other animal.

A spike-shaped metal fastener used for joining wood or similar materials. The nail is generally driven through two or more layers of material by means of impacts from a hammer or other device. It is then held in place by friction.
A round pedestal on which merchants once carried out their business, such as the four nails outside The Exchange, Bristol.
An archaic English unit of length equivalent to 1/20 of an ell or 1/16 of a yard (2 1/4 inches or 5.715 cm).
To fix (an object) to another object using a nail.
definitions.example: He nailed the placard to the post.

definitions.definition: To drive a nail.
definitions.example: He used the ax head for nailing.

definitions.definition: To stud or boss with nails, or as if with nails.
To catch.
To expose as a sham.
To accomplish (a task) completely and successfully.
definitions.example: I really nailed that test.

definitions.definition: To hit (a target) effectively with some weapon.
Of a male, to engage in sexual intercourse with.
To spike, as a cannon.
To nail down: to make certain, or confirm.
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
One who researches.
A state of safety, protection or shelter.
A place providing safety, protection or shelter.
Something or someone turned to for safety or assistance; a recourse or resort.
An expedient to secure protection or defence.
A refuge island.
To return to a place of shelter.
To shelter; to protect.
A repeated action; a doing again, refurbishment, etc.
To do again.
A reflected sound that is heard again by its initial observer.
An utterance repeating what has just been said.
A device in verse in which a line ends with a word which recalls the sound of the last word of the preceding line.
Sympathetic recognition; response; answer.
The displaying on the command line of the command that has just been executed.
The letter E in the ICAO spelling alphabet.

(whist) A signal, played in the same manner as a trump signal, made by a player who holds four or more trumps (or, as played by some, exactly three trumps) and whose partner has led trumps or signalled for trumps.
(whist) A signal showing the number held of a plain suit when a high card in that suit is led by one's partner.
(of a sound or sound waves) To reflect off a surface and return.
To reflect back (a sound).
(by extension) To repeat (another's speech, opinion etc.).

definitions.definition: To repeat its input as input to some other device or system.
(whist) To give the echo signal, informing one's partner about cards one holds.
The visual image formed by an echocardiograph.
The use of ultrasound to produce images of the heart.
A series of rulers or dynasts from one family.
A team or organization which has an extended period of success or dominant performance.
To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.
A brick, stone or cement floor to a fireplace or oven.
An open recess in a wall at the base of a chimney where a fire may be built.
The lowest part of a metallurgical furnace.
A brazier, chafing dish, or firebox.
Home or family life.
A household or group in some forms of the modern pagan faith Heathenry.
A person who considers it their own responsibility to uphold the law in their neighborhood and often does so summarily and without legal jurisdiction.
The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.
definitions.example: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.

definitions.definition: The degree of importance given to something.
definitions.example: The value of my children's happiness is second only to that of my wife.

definitions.definition: That which is valued or highly esteemed, such as one's morals, morality, or belief system.
definitions.example: He does not share his parents' values.

definitions.definition: The amount (of money or goods or services) that is considered to be a fair equivalent for something else.
The relative duration of a musical note.
definitions.example: The value of a crotchet is twice that of a quaver.

definitions.definition: The relative darkness or lightness of a color in (a specific area of) a painting etc.
Any definite numerical quantity or other mathematical object, determined by being measured, computed, or otherwise defined.
definitions.example: The exact value of pi cannot be represented in decimal notation.

definitions.definition: Precise meaning; import.
definitions.example: the value of a word; the value of a legal instrument

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The valuable ingredients to be obtained by treating a mass or compound; specifically, the precious metals contained in rock, gravel, etc.
definitions.example: The vein carries good values.

definitions.definition: Esteem; regard.
Valour; also spelled valew.
To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: Any of several carrion-eating birds of the families Accipitridae and Cathartidae.
A person who profits from the suffering of others.
definitions.example: Within ten minutes of the accident, the vultures appeared and were organizing lawsuits.

definitions.definition: To circle around one's target as if one were a vulture.
definitions.example: Rudy vultured when asking the girl out.

definitions.definition: The name of the Latin-script letter U.
A thing in the shape of the letter U
(abbreviation) you (in text messaging and internet conversations)
definitions.example: Take me with u.

definitions.definition: Beneath the surface of the water, or of or pertaining to the region beneath the water surface
Beneath the water line of a vessel
Under water.
Having negative equity; owing more on an asset than its market value
definitions.example: We've been underwater on our mortgage ever since the housing crash.

definitions.definition: A fold or wrinkle.
A state of perplexity or anxiety; confusion; bother; agitation.
To pinch or wrinkle; to squeeze inwardly, to dimple or fold.
The hole in a lock where the key is inserted and turns.
Any small opening resembling the hole for a key in shape or function.
A circle cut out of a garment as a decorative effect, typically at the front or back neckline of a dress.
A hole or excavation in beams intended to be joined together, to receive the key that fastens them.
A mortise for a key or cotter.
(lasers) A transient column of vapor or plasma formed when using high energy beams, such as lasers, for welding or cutting.
definitions.example: keyhole plasma

definitions.definition: A welding method in which a hole forms in the surface immediately ahead of the puddle in the direction of welding. The hole is filled as the weld progresses.
The free-throw lane together with the circle surrounding the free-throw line; key.
A gravitational keyhole.

(ordnance) To strike a target after wobbling in flight so that the long axis of the bullet does not follow the line of flight; typically due to insufficient spin resulting from the rifling in the barrel.
A little tower, frequently a merely ornamental structure at one of the corners of a building or castle.

A siege tower; a movable building, of a square form, consisting of ten or even twenty stories and sometimes one hundred and twenty cubits high, usually moved on wheels, and employed in approaching a fortified place, for carrying soldiers, engines, ladders, casting bridges, and other necessaries.
A tower-like solder post on a turret board (a circuit board with posts instead of holes).
An armoured, rotating gun installation on a fort, ship, aircraft, or armoured fighting vehicle.
The elevated central portion of the roof of a passenger car, with sides that are pierced for light and ventilation.
On a wind instrument, to articulate a note by starting the air with a tap of the tongue, as though by speaking a 'd' or 't' sound (alveolar plosive).
definitions.example: Playing wind instruments involves tonguing on the reed or mouthpiece.

definitions.definition: To manipulate with the tongue, as in kissing or oral sex.
To protrude in relatively long, narrow sections.
definitions.example: a soil horizon that tongues into clay

definitions.definition: To join by means of a tongue and groove.
definitions.example: to tongue boards together

definitions.definition: To talk; to prate.
To speak; to utter.
To chide; to scold.
Resembling a tongue.
(in combination) Having a particular manner of speaking.
The condition of being held in a state between two or more forces, which are acting in opposition to each other.
Psychological state of being tense.
A feeling of nervousness, excitement, or fear that is created in a movie, book, etc.; suspense.
State of an elastic object which is stretched in a way which increases its length.

Force transmitted through a rope, string, cable, or similar object (used with prepositions on, in, or of, e.g., "The tension in the cable is 1000 N", to convey that the same magnitude of force applies to objects attached to both ends).

Voltage. Usually only the terms low tension, high tension, and extra-high tension, and the abbreviations LT, HT, and EHT are used. They are not precisely defined; LT is normally a few volts, HT a few hundreds of volts, and EHT thousands of volts.
To place an object in tension, to pull or place strain on.
definitions.example: We tensioned the cable until it snapped.

definitions.definition: A sunfish.
(of weather or a day) Featuring a lot of sunshine.
definitions.example: Whilst it may be sunny today, the weather forecast is predicting rain.

definitions.definition: (of a place) Receiving a lot of sunshine.
definitions.example: I would describe Spain as sunny, but it's nothing in comparison to the Sahara.

definitions.definition: (of a person or a person's mood) cheerful
definitions.example: a sunny disposition

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the sun; proceeding from, or resembling the sun; shiny; radiant.
Sunny side up
A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.
A drudge; one who labors or is obliged (e.g. by prior contract) to labor like a slave with limited rights, e.g. an indentured servant.
An abject person.
One who has no power of resistance (to something), one who surrenders to or is under the domination (of something).
definitions.example: a slave to passion, to strong drink, or to ambition

definitions.definition: (BDSM) A submissive partner in a BDSM relationship who (consensually) submits to (sexually and/or personally) serving one or more masters or mistresses.
A sex slave, a person who is forced against their will to perform, for another person or group, sexual acts on a regular or continuing basis.
A device (such as a secondary flash or hard drive) that is subject to the control of another (a master).
To work as a slaver, to enslave people.
To work hard.
definitions.example: I was slaving all day over a hot stove.

definitions.definition: To place a device under the control of another.
definitions.example: to slave a hard disk

definitions.definition: A noisy disturbance and/or commotion.
A row, fight.
A disc jockey.
To perform as a disc jockey.
Rudely abrupt, unfriendly.
One who bonks.
Mad; crazy.
Someone who has represented their country in a particular sport.
definitions.example: The United team includes five England internationals.

definitions.definition: A game or contest between two or more nations.
A transnational organization of political parties of similar ideology.
Of or having to do with more than one nation.
Between or among nations
definitions.example: an international discussion

definitions.definition: Participated in by two or more nations
definitions.example: an international competition

definitions.definition: Common to, or affecting, two or more nations.
definitions.example: an international rule

definitions.definition: Serving two or more nations
definitions.example: an international airport

definitions.definition: Of or concerning the association called the International.
Independent of national boundaries; common to all people.
definitions.example: The atmosphere is an international resource.

definitions.definition: Foreign; of another nation.
definitions.example: an international student

definitions.definition: A viral infection, caused by Human alphaherpesvirus 1 and Human alphaherpesvirus 2, marked by painful, watery blisters in the skin or mucous membranes or on the genitals.
A turbofan engine.
An airplane powered by turbofan engines.
A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
A receptacle for oil in a lamp.
Spring, source, fountain.
A headfirst fall or jump.
A rapid fall in price or value.
(of aircraft) To dive down in a steep angle.
To perform a rapid fall in price or value.
Significantly; very, very much
Mostly, primarily
The transpose of the cofactor matrix of a given square matrix.
(of a matrix) Transpose conjugate.
(of an operator) Hermitian conjugate.
A functor related to another functor by an adjunction.

A curve A such that any point of a given curve C of multiplicity r has multiplicity at least r–1 on A. Sometimes the multiple points of C are required to be ordinary, and if this condition is not satisfied the term sub-adjoint is used.
An assistant to someone who holds a position in the military or civil service.
An assistant mayor of a French commune.
Used in certain contexts, in each case involving a pair of transformations, one of which is, or is analogous to, conjugation (either inner automorphism or complex conjugation).
(of a functor) That is related to another functor by an adjunction.
(of one curve to another curve) Having a relationship of the nature of an adjoint (adjoint curve); sharing multiple points with.
Something or somebody considered unlikely.
Not likely; improbable; not to be reasonably expected.
definitions.example: It's very unlikely that you'll be able to walk perfectly after being in a cast for six months.

definitions.definition: Not holding out a prospect of success; likely to fail; unpromising.
definitions.example: unlikely means

definitions.definition: In an improbable manner.
A type of music and dance that originated in the West Indies (perhaps Trinidad), characterized by improvised lyrics on topical or broadly humorous subjects, often creating satire of current events.
To perform calypso.
An act or episode of rescuing, saving.
A liberation, freeing.
The forcible ending of a siege; liberation from similar military peril
definitions.example: The rescue of Jerusalem was the original motive of the Crusaders

definitions.definition: A special airliner flight to bring home passengers who are stranded
A rescuee.
definitions.example: The dog was a rescue with some behavior issues.

definitions.definition: To save from any violence, danger or evil.
definitions.example: The well-trained team rescued everyone after the avalanche.

definitions.definition: To free or liberate from confinement or other physical restraint.
definitions.example: to rescue a prisoner from the enemy.

definitions.definition: To recover forcibly.
To deliver by arms, notably from a siege.
To remove or withdraw from a state of exposure to evil and sin.
definitions.example: Traditionally missionaries aim to rescue many ignorant heathen souls.

definitions.definition: To achieve something positive under difficult conditions.
To remove air or some other gas from within an elastic container, e.g. a balloon or tyre
To cause an object to decrease or become smaller in some parameter, e.g. to shrink
To reduce the amount of available currency or credit and thus lower prices.
To become deflated.
To let down or disappoint.
To compress (data) according to a particular algorithm.
Boisterous activity or behaviour, especially in sport; lively fun.
Tricky or waggish behavior; mischief.
An old Scottish parlour game in which people played various parts under penalty of a forfeit.
A painted tin-glazed ceramic tile of a kind found in Spain and Portugal, often arranged to form geometric patterns.
Stealthy.
Exhibiting guilty or evasive secrecy.
One who finds or discovers something.
An optical device, such as a viewfinder, used to locate a target or other object of interest
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
An instance of stabbing with a knife.
definitions.example: There was a knifing outside that club last night.

definitions.definition: A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
A fulfilment of a need or desire.
definitions.example: He enjoyed the dish with great satisfaction. He'll order it again the next time he arrives.

definitions.definition: The pleasure obtained by such fulfillment.
The source of such gratification.
A reparation for an injury or loss.
A vindication for a wrong suffered.
definitions.example: The count demanded satisfaction in the form of a duel at dawn.

definitions.definition: A feeling of illness or discomfort in the digestive system, usually characterized by a strong urge to vomit.
Strong dislike or disgust.
Motion sickness.
Having or displaying ill will; wishing harm on others
Having an evil or harmful influence
Any device used to jam radio reception.
A form of swimwear used by athletes.
A musician who jams.
A device (e.g. a jumar) which will slide along a rope in one direction but not the other, used in rock-climbing, caving etc.
A player who attempts to score points by making their way past other players.
A midday meal (in a context in which the evening meal is called supper or tea).
The main meal of the day, often eaten in the evening.
An evening meal.
A meal given to an animal.
definitions.example: Give the dog its dinner.

definitions.definition: A formal meal for many people eaten for a special occasion.
The food provided or consumed at any such meal.
To eat a dinner.
To provide (someone) with a dinner.
To cheer with a huzzah sound.
Done by one side only.
Affecting only one side of the body.
Binding or affecting one party only.
A close hand-to-hand struggle; the act of grappling.
To seize something and hold it firmly.
To wrestle or tussle.
(with with) To ponder and intensely evaluate a problem.
definitions.example: to grapple with one's conscience

definitions.definition: The act of gaining; acquisition.
What is gained.
The factor by which a signal is multiplied.
To acquire possession of.
definitions.example: Looks like you've gained a new friend.

definitions.definition: To have or receive advantage or profit; to acquire gain; to grow rich; to advance in interest, health, or happiness; to make progress.
definitions.example: The sick man gains daily.

definitions.definition: To come off winner or victor in; to be successful in; to obtain by competition.
definitions.example: to gain a battle; to gain a case at law

definitions.definition: To increase.
To be more likely to catch or overtake an individual.
definitions.example: I'm gaining (on you).

definitions.definition: To reach.
definitions.example: to gain the top of a mountain

definitions.definition: To draw into any interest or party; to win to one's side; to conciliate.
To put on weight.
definitions.example: I've been gaining.

definitions.definition: (of a clock or watch) To run fast.
A square or bevelled notch cut out of a girder, binding joist, or other timber which supports a floor beam, so as to receive the end of the floor beam.
A combination of events which have come together by chance to make a surprisingly good or wonderful outcome.
An unsought, unintended, and/or unexpected, but fortunate, discovery and/or learning experience that happens by accident.
The steroid hormone hydrocortisone.
Personal charm or magnetism
An extraordinary power granted by the Holy Spirit
The ability to influence without the use of logic.
An escape from prison.
To escape from prison.
To modify the firmware of an electronic device, especially a mobile phone, in order to remove restrictions that prevent it from running unofficial software.
A mineral consisting, like quartz, of silica, but inferior to quartz in hardness and specific gravity, of the chemical formula SiO2·nH2O.
A colloquial name used in molecular biology referring to a particular stop codon sequence, "UGA."
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Nesolycaena.
One who destroys religious images or icons, especially an opponent of the Orthodox Church in the 8th and 9th centuries, or a Puritan during the European Reformation.
One who opposes orthodoxy and religion; one who adheres to the doctrine of iconoclasm.
(by extension) One who attacks cherished beliefs.
Abounding in words, containing more words than necessary; long-winded.
Producing unusually detailed output for diagnostic purposes.
A length of thread, line or rope that is doubled over to make an opening.
The opening so formed.
A shape produced by a curve that bends around and crosses itself.
definitions.example: Arches, loops, and whorls are patterns found in fingerprints.

definitions.definition: A ring road or beltway.
An endless strip of tape or film allowing continuous repetition.
A complete circuit for an electric current.
A programmed sequence of instructions that is repeated until or while a particular condition is satisfied.
An edge that begins and ends on the same vertex.
A path that starts and ends at the same point.
A bus or rail route, walking route, etc. that starts and ends at the same point.
A place at a terminus where trains or trams can turn round and go back the other way without having to reverse; a balloon loop, turning loop, or reversing loop.
A quasigroup with an identity element.
A loop-shaped intrauterine device.
An aerobatic maneuver in which an aircraft flies a circular path in a vertical plane.
A small, narrow opening; a loophole.
A flexible region in a protein's secondary structure.
A mass of iron in a pasty condition gathered into a ball for the tilt hammer or rolls.
A young goat.
definitions.example: He treated the oxen like they didn't exist, but he treated the goat kid like a puppy.

definitions.definition: Of a female goat, the state of being pregnant: in kid.
Kidskin.
The meat of a young goat.
A young antelope.
A child (usually), teenager, or young adult; a juvenile.
definitions.example: She's a kid. It's normal for her to have imaginary friends.

definitions.definition: A person whose childhood took place in a particular time period or area.
definitions.example: He's been living in Los Angeles for years now, but he's a Florida kid.

definitions.definition: One's son or daughter, regardless of age.
definitions.example: He was their youngest kid.

definitions.definition: (in the vocative) Used as a form of address for a child, teenager or young adult
definitions.example: No, kid, you didn't do anything wrong; they did!

definitions.definition: An inexperienced person or one in a junior position.
A deception; an act of kidding somebody.
A small wooden mess tub in which sailors received their food.
To make a fool of (someone).
To dupe or deceive (someone).
To make a joke with (someone).
Of a goat, to give birth to kids.
To joke.
definitions.example: Only kidding

definitions.definition: A fagot; a bundle of heath and furze.
To apply a physical texture to.
definitions.example: Synthetic food can be texturized to make it resemble meat.

definitions.definition: To apply a visual texture to.
definitions.example: I have texturized this sphere with a woodgrain pattern.

definitions.definition: A pill, a tablet.
To arrange in tabular form; to arrange into a table.
To set out as a list; to enumerate, to list.
To enter into an official register or roll.
To shape with a flat surface.
Scumbag; scuzzball
A space on a paper form that can be optionally filled with a check mark.
An on-screen box that can be optionally filled with a check mark, to enable or disable a setting.
Casually calm and relaxed.
definitions.example: We handled the whole frenetic situation with a nonchalant attitude.

definitions.definition: Indifferent; unconcerned; behaving as if detached.
definitions.example: He is far too nonchalant about such a serious matter.

definitions.definition: To stir together.
definitions.example: Mix the eggs and milk with the flour until the consistency is smooth.

definitions.definition: To combine (items from two or more sources normally kept separate).
definitions.example: Don't mix the meat recipes with the dairy recipes.

definitions.definition: To form by mingling; to produce by the stirring together of ingredients; to concoct from different parts.
definitions.example: Yellow and blue paint mix to make green.

definitions.definition: To blend by the use of a mixer (machine).
definitions.example: Mix the egg whites until they are stiff.

definitions.definition: To combine (several tracks).
definitions.example: I'll mix the rhythm tracks down to a single track.

definitions.definition: To produce a finished version of (a recording).
definitions.example: I'm almost done mixing this song.

definitions.definition: To unite with in company; to join; to associate.
Having two or more separate aspects.
definitions.example: I get a very mixed feeling from this puzzling painting.

definitions.definition: Not completely pure, tainted or adulterated.
definitions.example: My joy was somewhat mixed when my partner said she was pregnant: it's a lot of responsibility.

definitions.definition: Including both male(s) and female(s).
definitions.example: My son attends a mixed school, my daughter an all-girl grammar school.

definitions.definition: Stemming from two or more races or breeds
definitions.example: Mixed blood can surprisingly produce inherited properties which neither parent showed

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or connected with muscles.
Brawny, thewy, having strength.
Having large, well-developed muscles.
Robust, strong.
Men's or boys' briefs.
A closely-cropped military-style haircut.
A fencer's practice foil with a soft tip.
A short needle with a strong point.
(smoking) A marijuana cigar.
definitions.example: 2005: to make his point, lead rapper B-Real fired up a blunt in front of the cameras and several hundred thousand people and announced, “I'm taking a hit for every one of y'all!” — Martin Torgoff, Can't Find My Way Home (Simon & Schuster 2005, p. 461)

definitions.definition: Money
A playboating move resembling a cartwheel performed on a wave.
Having a thick edge or point; not sharp.
Dull in understanding; slow of discernment; opposed to acute.
Abrupt in address; plain; unceremonious; wanting the forms of civility; rough in manners or speech.
definitions.example: the blunt admission that he had never liked my company

definitions.definition: Hard to impress or penetrate.
Slow or deficient in feeling: insensitive.

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.
definitions.example: The rough waters of change will bring about the calm after the storm.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
A protective garment worn on a knee to protect it from injury, for example due to a blow or a fall.
Impudent; impertinent; impertinently bold, often in a way that is regarded as endearing or amusing.
(of swimwear, underwear, etc.) tending to reveal the cheeks of the buttocks.
(Australian Aboriginal) Poisonous (of animals such as snakes), dangerous, cunning, violent, potent.
Indulged in.

The use of a word or phrase to refer to something that it is not, invoking a direct similarity between the word or phrase used and the thing described (but in the case of English without the words like or as, since use of those words would imply a simile); the word or phrase used in this way; an implied comparison.
The use of an everyday object or concept to represent an underlying facet of the computer and thus aid users in performing tasks.
definitions.example: desktop metaphor; wastebasket metaphor

definitions.definition: To use a metaphor.
To describe by means of a metaphor.
To secrete saliva, especially in anticipation of food.
To secrete any substance in a similar way.
definitions.example: The alien beast drooled slime.

definitions.definition: To react to something with uncontrollable desire.
definitions.example: That boy is so attractive I drool whenever I see him!

definitions.definition: To talk nonsense; drivel.
The act of one who drools.
